folio.label: 101v
folio.l: HbouenAbouenHAboueneuäy , worldly creature826Double check this corrections. Notice it appeared at the beginning of the verso, check consistency with progress of copying.ODR
¢ That ©e so longe , of ©oure benignite
Han holden me , in honour and nobley
Where as I was , nougút worthy for to be
That thanke I god , and ©ow , to whom I prey
For ©elde it ©ow , there is nomore to sey
Vn to my fader , gladly wol I wende
And witú hi~ dwelle , vn to my lyues ende
¢ There I was fostered , of a child ful smal
Til I be dede , my lyf there wol I lede
A wydewe clene , in body hert and al
And sithe I ©af to ©ow , my maydenhede
And am ©oure trewe wyf , it is no drede
God shilde , swicú a lordes wyf , to take
A nother man , to husbonde or to make
¢ And of ©oure newe wyf , god of his grace
So graunte ©ow welthe , and ¸s¹ite
For I wol gladly , ©elden hire my place
In whicú that I was , blisful wont to be
For sithe it liketú ©ow , my lord q¶ she
That whilom weren , al myn hertis rest
That I shal gon , I wyl go whan ©ow list
¢ But there as ©e me ¸fre , swicú doware
As I first brougút , it is wel in my mynde
It were my wreched clothes , no thyngö faire
The whicú to me , were hard now for to fynde
O goode god , . how gentyl and how kynde
ñe semed by ©oure speche , and ©oure vysage
The day , that maked was , oure mariage
¢ But sotú is seid , algate I fynde it trewe
For in effect , it preued is on me
Loue is nougút old , as whan that it is newe
But certes lord , for noon aduäsitee
To deye in this cas , it shal nat be
That euäe in word or werk , I shal repent
That I ©ow ©af myn hert , in hole entent
¢ My lord ©e wot , that in my fadres place
ñe dide me stripe , out of my poore wede
And richely me clad , of ©oure grace
To ©ow brougút I nat ellis , out of drede
But filthe and nakednesse , and maydenhede
And heere a geyn , my clothyngö I restore
And eke my weddyngö ryngö , for euäemore
¢ The remennt of ©oure Ioweles , redy be
With inne ©oure chaumbre , I dar it safly seyn
Naked out of my fadres hous , q¶ she
